reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output omits a massive portion of the source document. Specifically, pages 4 through approximately 118 are missing, which include essential legal terms (Sections B through EE), the body of the Master Agreement (Attachment A), and several key attachments (B-1, B-2, C-1, D-1). The output explicitly notes that 115 sections were omitted due to token budget constraints, resulting in a catastrophic loss of content fidelity for a legal contract.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document contains Sections B through EE (pages 4-22) and multiple attachments spanning over 100 pages.
concerns.location: Between sections at element index 43 and 55

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The section 'NC eProcurement System' is cut off mid-sentence after the introductory paragraph. The source document contains another full page of text (points 1 through 4) detailing the specific requirements for this system, meaning more than 25% of this individual section's content is missing.

concerns.location: NC eProcurement System section, element index 65

concerns.analysis: The source document on page 24 contains a side-by-side (2-column) signature block, where the left column contains the details for the 'Participating Entity' and the right column contains the details for the 'Contractor'. In the HTML output, this layout is collapsed into a single vertical sequence of properties. concerns.output_quote: The HTML renders a single table 'Signature Section' where the values are listed sequentially: Participating Entity, Contractor, Signature, Signature, Name, Name, etc.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: In the 'Contractor Worker Location' table, the output fails to capture the contractor's representation regarding work performed outside the U.S. In the source, the 'No' checkbox is marked with an 'X', whereas the output leaves the value empty. This omission changes the meaning of a contractual representation.

concerns.analysis: The table representing 'Contractor Utilization of Workers Outside the U.S.' (Section H on page 5) is misrendered in the HTML. In the source PDF, the user has explicitly selected 'No' (indicated by '_X_ No'). In the HTML output, the corresponding 'Value' cell for this field is empty (' '), failing to display the critical data selection present in the original document.
